Rooftop gardens can't replace lost horizons.
Nostalgia doesn't pay rent - stability vs history?
Honestly miss when cityscapes weren't dominated by glass towers — something earnestly charming about old architecture's character that no modern spot can replicate.
Kinda ironic how everyone talks up trendy bars when just below them neighborhoods are losing their soul to new developments no one asked for.
Feels like they're erasing cherished pages from our city’s storybook—swapping tales for steel beams.
Vows were my ultimate 'This is real' moment.
End up proofreading at restaurants – can't turn it off!
